name: Serper MCP Server (Google Search API)
what_it_does: Serper MCP server enabling AI agents to perform real-time Google Search via the Serper.dev API — searching the web, Google News, Google Images, Google Maps, and Google Shopping; retrieving current search results; and integrating real-time web search capabilities into agent-driven research, fact-checking, and information retrieval workflows.
use_cases: Searching current web content for research from information retrieval agents
Querying Google News for recent events from news monitoring agents
Searching Google Images for visual content discovery from media agents
Looking up local business information via Google Maps from location agents
Fact-checking claims against current web results from verification agents
Searching Google Shopping for product pricing from e-commerce agents
not_for: Teams needing organic SEO data (use Google Search Console MCP)
Teams using Bing or DuckDuckGo search (Serper is Google-only)
High-volume search at low cost (Serper charges per query)
best_when: An agent needs real-time Google Search results — for current events, fact-checking, web research, or news monitoring where training data is insufficient.
avoid_when: You need SEO analytics rather than search results, or if you're cost-sensitive at high search volumes — Serper charges per query.

auth.notes: Serper API key from serper.dev dashboard. Single API key for all search types (web, news, images, etc.). Key included in HTTP headers per request.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 2,500 free queries on signup; no ongoing free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: From $50/2500 queries to $100/10000 queries; enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Serper gives 2,500 free queries on signup. Beyond that, pay-per-query. Much cheaper than Google Custom Search API. Community MCP server is open source and free.

known_agent_gotchas: 2,500 free queries expire — agents must monitor query usage to avoid unexpected costs
Query results are Google Search results — not guaranteed to include all relevant pages
gl (country) and hl (language) params affect results — agents should set appropriately
Image search returns URLs that may be hosted on third-party sites (link rot risk)
Google SERP structure changes — Serper results may vary in format over time
Community MCP server — may not expose all Serper API parameters
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Single API key auth. GDPR. Community MCP server. Protect API key as secret. Monitor query consumption to control costs. Results include third-party URLs.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Single API key. GDPR. Community MCP server. Protect API key. Monitor usage for cost control.
